DISCOVERY 525, AUG-16-2024 RE: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMINISTRATIVE SUBPOENAS. NOT ARGUED/CONTESTED. THE COURT ACKNOWLEDGES THAT THERE IS NO APPEARANCE BY THE RESPONDENT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT HEREBY ORDERS RESPONDENT EZEQUIEL MONTANO LLC TO COMPLY WITH THE TWO ADMINISTRATIVE SUBPOENAS AT ISSUE HEREIN. SEE MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR PETITIONER IS DIRECTED TO PREPARE A FORM OF PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING AND SUBMIT IT TO PSW@HASSARD.COM FOR REVIEW AND EXECUTION. JUDGE PRO TEM: PHILIP WARD. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Administrative Subpoenas
Off Calendar Aug-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA ACTING BY AND THROUGH SAN FRANCISCO CITY ATTORNEY DAVID CHIU Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Administrative Subpoenas.

NANCY E. HARRIS, Esq., , in person, Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3957, for the CITY ATTORNEY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ACTING ON BEHALF OF THE PEOPLE OF CALIFORNIA, Petitioner.
No other appearances.

Not argued/contested. The court acknowledges that there is no appearance by the Respondent. The court adopts its tentative ruling. The City Attorney of San Francisco petitions for an order compelling Respondent to comply with administrative subpoenas in connection with an investigation being conducted by the City Attorney into suspected violations of the Unfair Competition Law, Business and Professions Code sections 17200, et seq. This action is the converse of Action No. CPF 24-518575 in which respondent Ezequiel Montano LLC is petitioning the Court to quash the same administrative subpoenas issued by the City Attorney. In Action No. CPF 24-518575, the Court has issued a tentative ruling denying the Petitioner's motion for an order quashing the two administrative subpoenas that are the subject of the instant action. Accordingly, for good cause shown, the Court hereby ORDERS respondent Ezequiel Montano LLC to comply with the two administrative subpoenas at issue herein. Respondent is ORDERED to produce the documents sought in the records subpoena issued by the City Attorney and to respond to the interrogatories posed in the second administrative subpoena. Compliance with both subpoenas shall be made no later than August 30, 2024. Although Petitioner also seeks an award of its costs incurred in the filing of its petition, the People (or the City) are exempt from paying filing fees, and the record is unclear as to what other costs are referred to in the Petition. For that reason, no costs are awarded.

Counsel for Petitioner is directed to prepare a form of proposed order consistent with the tentative ruling and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and execution.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Court Reporter: Brianna Rudd; CSR: 13668; T: 209.747.1306; E: briannarudd@gmail.com. (525/JPT)
